title: MANGA NGE Which volumes should I buy
display_title: MANGA NGE Which volumes should I buy
wikitext:
[]: [NGE] Which volumes should I buy?             Quote Post by Heidroch » Mon Nov 07, 2005 4:58 pm Hello- I've been an avid Eva fan for the past couple of years now, having watched the series and the movies a few times through. However, I have never read the Eva manga, or any manga for that matter. From the discussions on this board, it seems evident that the Eva manga is telling different variations of the story than the anime, and I'm interested in seeing what these variations are. So my question for you is: What volumes of the Eva manga do you reccomend I buy? I'm looking for the volumes that differ the most from the anime in their telling of certain parts of the story. The thing that is keeping me from buying the entire series is that I am a bit hesitant as to spending nearly $100 on a manga series that may for the most part be just like the anime. Any help would be appreciated!

[]:             Quote Post by akda1ndaonly1 » Mon Nov 07, 2005 11:31 pm The first four volumes of the book aren't all that different from the anime, its practically how the anime went. It starts to get a little different in volume 5 but skips a few angels. As in the story line, it stays somewhat similar. IMO, starting from this volume is where everything changes a bit. Volume 6 is entirely about Touji's fiasco with his Evangelion. And so on. So IMO, you should start buying from volume 5 because I think that is where the plot of the manga starts to diverge from the plot of the anime.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Nov 08, 2005 2:14 am The manga starts out slightly different, with Rei piloring an Eva against Sachiel, and Shinji seeing their battle while he's waiting for Misato - and diverges more as time passes. Asuka's arrival in vol4 is completely different (and so is Asuka, really).

[]:             Quote Post by Phoenix Feet » Tue Nov 08, 2005 9:47 am It's worth buying the lot, really. The basic plot follows the anime for the first 4, but there are a lot of small divergences, so it's not going to make total sense just going from volume 5. It's a good bit of storytelling by it's own right. Read it from the start.

[]: ^_^ \/,,             Quote Post by chiefen » Tue Nov 08, 2005 12:12 pm I'd say that the manga gets a much more dark feeling to it in the later volumes than the anime.

[]: Re: ^_^ \/,,             Quote Post by superdoughboy4 » Tue Nov 08, 2005 4:43 pm chiefen wrote: I'd say that the manga gets a much more dark feeling to it in the later volumes than the anime.

[]: Re: ^_^ \/,,             Quote Post by Space Penis » Sun Nov 27, 2005 2:49 pm superdoughboy4 wrote: chiefen wrote: I'd say that the manga gets a much more dark feeling to it in the later volumes than the anime.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Nov 27, 2005 7:16 pm None of the volumes are exactly the same, but with volume 4 you see some divergence. Especially 5, 8, and 9. 4, 6, and 7 have key plot points rewritten.
